query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Anxious', 'Greatly concerned, respecting something future or unknown', 'What seemed anxious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apartheid', 'Any system that separates people according to race', 'The author hoped the new policy would apartheid tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apathy', 'Lack of interest or concern', 'Her apathy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aperture', 'An opening, hole, or gap', 'Scholars noted a striking aperture between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apex', 'The top or highest point', 'Her apex became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aphorism', 'A pithy observation containing a general truth', 'Scholars noted a striking aphorism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apiary', 'A place where bees and their hives are kept', 'Her apiary became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Apocryphal', 'Of doubtful authenticity', 'The reviewer described the tone as apocryphal, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apogee', 'The point in orbit around Earth when the object is farthest', 'Scholars noted a striking apogee between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apolitical', 'Having no interest or involvement in politics', 'What seemed apolitical at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apostasy', 'The renunciation of a belief or set of beliefs', 'Her apostasy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apostate', 'A person who forsakes his religion, cause, party', 'Scholars noted a striking apostate between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apostle', 'A missionary, or leader of a religious mission', 'The essay highlights the apostle of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apothecary', 'A person who makes and provides/sells drugs and/or medicines', 'The essay highlights the apothecary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apothegm', 'A short, witty, instructive saying, an aphorism or maxim', 'Her apothegm became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apotheosis', 'The highest point in the development of something', 'The essay highlights the apotheosis of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appall', 'Causing dismay or horror', 'Her appall became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apparent', 'Capable of being seen, or easily seen', 'The passage presents a apparent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apparition', 'A supernatural appearance of a person or thing, like a ghost', 'Her apparition became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appease', 'To calm or satisfy someone', 'The author hoped the new policy would appease tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appeasement', 'The action or process of satisfying the demands of a potentially hostile person or group to maintain peace', 'Critics argued that the plan might appeasement the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appellate', 'Describing something that is appealed to', 'Critics argued that the plan might appellate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Append', 'To add as supplement or in addition', 'Critics argued that the plan might append the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appertain', 'To relate to', 'The author hoped the new policy would appertain tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Applicable', 'Suitable for application, relevant, appropriate', 'The passage presents a applicable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Application', 'The act of putting to a special use or purpose', 'Critics argued that the plan might application the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apportion', 'To divide and distribute portions of a whole', 'Over time, the community learned to apportion its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apposite', 'Appropriate, relevant, well-suited, fit', 'The essay highlights the apposite of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apprais', 'Assessing the value or quality of something', 'Her apprais became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appraise', 'To assess the value or quality of', 'Critics argued that the plan might appraise the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apprehend', 'To take or seize, to take hold of', 'Critics argued that the plan might apprehend the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apprehensive', 'Anxious or fearful about the future', 'The reviewer described the tone as apprehensive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apprentice', 'A person who''s learning a trade from a skilled employer', 'Scholars noted a striking apprentice between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apprise', 'To notify, or to make aware, to inform', 'Critics argued that the plan might apprise the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Approbation', 'Approval or praise', 'The essay highlights the approbation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appropriate', 'Suitable or fitting for a particular purpose', 'Critics argued that the plan might appropriate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Approximation', 'A rough estimate or near calculation', '“The approximation of the building’s height was around 50 feet.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Appurtenance', 'An appendage added to something else', 'Critics argued that the plan might appurtenance the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Apropo', 'Of an appropriate or pertinent nature', 'The essay highlights the apropo of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aptitude', 'A natural ability to do something', 'Her aptitude became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aquatic', 'Relating to water', 'Critics argued that the plan might aquatic the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aqueduct', 'A small canal containing fluid', 'Her aqueduct became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aqueous', 'Consisting mostly of water', 'The reviewer described the tone as aqueous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Arable', 'Suitable for cultivation, such as by ploughing', 'The passage presents a arable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Arbiter', 'A person empowered to make decisions', 'Critics argued that the plan might arbiter the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Arbitrary', 'Based on random choice rather than reason', 'The passage presents a arbitrary portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Arbitrate', 'To judge, to umpire', 'Over time, the community learned to arbitrate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Arbitrator', 'A person delegated the authority to settle a dispute', 'The author hoped the new policy would arbitrator tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Arbor', 'A shady sitting place, usually in a park or garden', 'Scholars noted a striking arbor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
